Incremental racemization in power-law space

Source: R/kinetics.R
dRacPL.Rd

Computes the increment in \((D/L)^x\) accumulated over one timestep at rate constant kt. Racemization is integrated in power-law \((D/L)^x\) space (the Bada linearization) rather than D/L space, because \((D/L)^x\) increases linearly with \(k_T t\) and avoids the sigmoidal curvature that makes D/L accumulation rate-dependent on the current value.

Usage

dRacPL(kt, delta.yr)

Arguments

kt

Numeric. Rate constant from arrhenius.

delta.yr

Numeric. Timestep in years.

Value

Numeric vector of incremental \((D/L)^x\) values.

Examples

kt <- arrhenius(Temp = 278)
dRacPL(kt, delta.yr = 10)
#> [1] 3.362735e-06
